mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 8% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 8%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates expanding demand conditions that lift equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show negative correlation with combined GDPC1/HOUST growth in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average signals improving supply chain efficiency that reduces corporate cost pressures, driving positive SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average in 60%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2% combined with stable FEDFUNDS signals inflation persistence that forces central bank rate hikes, producing negative SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show positive correlation with CPI/FEDFUNDS co-movements in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k net additions signals weakening labor demand that reduces corporate profit expectations, driving negative SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 70%+ of instances where PAYEMS falls below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates deteriorating consumer demand that pressures equity earnings, producing negative SPX returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show positive correlation with RSAFS/UNRATE co-movements in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 100 combined with declining oil prices (not in catalog) would signal strong supply growth, but since oil prices aren't available, this hypothesis uses INDPRO alone as a proxy for supply-side strength driving SPX positive returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show negative correlation with INDPRO growth in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < -0.05).
